skill.name: paypal-integration
skill.description: Integrate PayPal payment processing with support for express checkout, subscriptions, and refund management. Use when implementing PayPal payments, processing online transactions, or building e-commerce payment flows.
skill.summary: Complete PayPal integration skill for express checkout, recurring billing, IPN handling, and refund workflows with production-ready code examples.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation file (SKILL.md) containing legitimate PayPal payment integration code examples. The 'external_commands' patterns are markdown code blocks, not executable shell commands. The 'network' patterns are official PayPal API endpoints for payment processing. No security vulnerabilities detected.

content.value_statement: Add secure PayPal payment processing to your e-commerce application with support for express checkout, recurring billing, and automated refund handling. This skill provides production-ready code examples and best practices for PayPal API integration.
actual_capabilities: Implement PayPal Express Checkout with Smart Payment Buttons
Create and manage subscription plans for recurring billing
Process IPN (Instant Payment Notification) webhooks securely
Handle full and partial refunds programmatically
Verify PayPal API responses and handle errors
limitations: Requires valid PayPal Business account credentials
Does not support alternative payment processors like Stripe
IPN handling requires a publicly accessible webhook endpoint
Subscription features require PayPal product creation first

use_cases.title: E-commerce checkout integration
use_cases.description: Add PayPal as a payment option to your online store with express checkout flow, allowing customers to pay without leaving your site.

use_cases.title: SaaS subscription billing
use_cases.description: Implement recurring billing for SaaS products using PayPal subscription plans with automatic renewals and payment failure handling.

use_cases.title: Marketplace payout system
use_cases.description: Build a payout system to send payments to multiple recipients, useful for marketplaces and platform businesses.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PayPal button setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add PayPal Smart Payment Buttons to my checkout page. I have a PayPal Business account and need to accept one-time payments in USD.

prompt_templates.title: Subscription plan creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a monthly subscription plan for my SaaS product at $29/month. Include the API calls to create the plan and activate subscriptions.

prompt_templates.title: IPN webhook handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an IPN webhook handler for my Express.js backend that verifies PayPal notifications and updates order status in my database.

prompt_templates.title: Refund workflow automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement an automated refund system that processes customer refund requests, validates eligibility, and creates PayPal refunds with audit logging.

output_examples.input: Create a PayPal checkout button for a $50 product
output_examples.output: A complete HTML and JavaScript implementation with PayPal Smart Buttons, including client-side order creation, approval handling, and server-side capture verification with proper error handling.

output_examples.input: How do I verify IPN messages are from PayPal?
output_examples.output: Step-by-step IPN verification process: 1) Receive POST data from PayPal, 2) Add cmd=_notify-validate parameter, 3) POST back to ipnpb.paypal.com, 4) Check for VERIFIED response, 5) Process only verified notifications with duplicate transaction checks.

best_practices: Always verify IPN messages by posting back to PayPal before processing any payment notification
Use sandbox environment for testing and switch to production credentials only after thorough testing
Implement idempotent transaction processing to handle duplicate IPN notifications safely
anti_patterns: Processing IPN notifications without verification - allows fraudulent payment confirmations
Storing PayPal credentials in source code instead of environment variables
Mixing sandbox and production API endpoints or credentials in the same environment

faq.question: Do I need a PayPal Business account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a PayPal Business account to access the PayPal REST API and receive payments. Personal accounts cannot accept API-based payments.

faq.question: What is the difference between sandbox and production mode?
faq.answer: Sandbox mode uses test credentials and fake transactions for development. Production mode processes real payments. Always test thoroughly in sandbox before switching to production.

faq.question: How do I handle webhook failures?
faq.answer: Implement retry logic on PayPal side and ensure your webhook endpoint returns 200 status quickly. Log all webhook events for debugging and reconciliation.

faq.question: Can I customize the PayPal checkout experience?
faq.answer: Yes, you can customize button styles, enable guest checkout, specify payment methods, and configure the return URL experience using the application_context parameters.

faq.question: What happens if a subscription payment fails?
faq.answer: PayPal automatically retries failed payments based on your plan configuration. You can set payment_failure_threshold to specify how many retries before suspension.

faq.question: How do I test refunds without using real money?
faq.answer: Use sandbox test accounts to create and capture test orders, then process refunds against those sandbox transactions. No real money is involved in sandbox mode.
